raw_consumer_statement:
This is the 2nd half of an existing dispute. This was for the amount if {$850.00} ALSO not handled properly!!

I sent a billing dispute to Citibank regarding 2 charges ( both on XX/XX/year>XXXX ) for the same company, XXXX XXXX ( payment was made in 2 payments to them, one in XX/XX/year>XXXX  and one in XX/XX/year> XXXX - {$1200.00} & XXXX - {$850.00} ), for goods and services not as expected. Along with this, I sent over 60 pages of evidence to back up my dispute claim, which included text messages back and forth between myself and XXXX XXXX XXXX, receipts, photos, and I tried to send a video, but they don't accept those. I called the next day and confirmed that all evidence was received and was told it was and that I would hear from someone within 90 days, that it usually takes a while to go through everything. I said that was fine. Yesterday, XX/XX/XXXX ( Not even THIRTY days after I submitted my dispute ), my dispute gets resolved IN THE FAVOR OF THE MERCHANT. On the dispute resolution notice it said " The documentation or facts provided do not support the billing error dispute claim '' 1 ) They never contacted the merchant ( There usually is something that says we are contacting the merchant and will get back to you - there wasn't ) and 2 ) this resolution came BEFORE 30 days, so how could they have gone over everything, discussed it with the merchant and then got back to me? It is my feeling that they did NOT look at any evidence and just decided they wanted to keep the money with citibank. They reversed my conditional credit and will now be collecting further interest on this, as when they reversed it the accrual of interest stopped. I filed an appeal with them and have heard NOTHING. I want this resolved fairly and do not feel this has been done
